source_pr.title: session: increase default lock ttl to stabilize pessimistic test suite
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: zyguan <zhongyangguan@gmail.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/30677

Problem Summary:

Some tests within `testPessimisticSuite` are unstable because sometimes locks are gone unexpectedly. After some investigations we found that the lock ttl is set to 300ms by default, thus the problem may caused by the jitter of the environment (many slowlog items are seen).

Related CI links:
- https://ci.pingcap.net/blue/organizations/jenkins/tidb_ghpr_check_2/detail/tidb_ghpr_check_2/63945/pipeline/
- https://ci.pingcap.net/blue/organizations/jenkins/tidb_ghpr_check_2/detail/tidb_ghpr_check_2/63783/pipeline/

### What is changed and how it works?

Increase default lock ttl used in `testPessimisticSuite`. The previous 300ms TTL is introduced by https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/11037 for `TestOptimisticConflicts`, however we already use [1s](https://github.com/tier-cap/tidb/blob/88f57113ce0550584fded1d9ee77bc61d23e2650/session/pessimistic_test.go#L439-L442) for that case currently. Besides, many tests within `testPessimisticSuite` also set the ttl to 3s or 5s explicitly. Thus, this PR increase it to 5s by default.
